page: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1 instruction cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a 614 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No dedicated GPUs are included.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2702.98. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 21616.8 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9880.0. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 1031.04 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 39.01 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in large-block memory write bandwidth, which is weak at 22.73 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested hardware.

For application workloads, the D16ads_v5 demonstrates average capabilities across database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. Redis SET operations achieve 16.0 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 17.46 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds for a small 135M model are strong for text generation at 269.26 tokens/sec, though prompt processing remains average. This instance offers balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database applications requiring local temporary storage, presenting a standard cost-to-performance ratio for mid-tier enterprise workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 8 physical cores, it includes a bundled 614 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, scoring 2702.98 in stress-ng single-core tests, while multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth remain average. A key tradeoff is its weak large-block memory write bandwidth of 22.73 GB/sec. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, and static web serving, delivering balanced resource density for enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 614 GB local disk, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 614 GB local disk, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, integrated local storage, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and a 614 GB local disk
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Weak large-block memory write bandwidth of 22.73 GB/sec
Suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, and web serving
